extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. Only transition t4 sets sent:=true while opted_out is true; it fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and acc==10). So the violation requires reaching opted_out=true with acc==10, then sending. The step transitions t5/t6/t7 each compute acc := (3*acc + k) mod 16 where k=1 (step_0), k=5 (step_1), k=3 (step_2); the triple-nested if-then-else just subtracts 16 up to three times, which fully reduces mod 16 since 3*15+5=50 < 64. From acc=0, step_2 gives (0*3+3)%16=3, then step_0 gives (3*3+1)%16=10. acc=10 is thus reachable. Sequence: [step_2, step_0] reaches acc=10 (opted_out still false, sent false); [optout] sets opted_out=true, sent=false (t1); [send_attempt] then matches t4 (opted_out and acc==10) and sets sent:=true, yielding state opted_out=true AND sent=true. This 4-event execution (well under the 18-event bound) reaches a state where opted_out is true but sent is true, violating the property. Simulation confirms final state (opted_out=True, sent=True, acc=10).
